sources.title: SRF Stock Forecast - Simply Wall St
SRF Q3 FY26 Results - SRF Official

data.summary: SRF Limited is a multi-business specialty chemicals conglomerate with a leadership position in Indian fluorochemicals. The investment thesis centres on a structural shift from commodity HFCs to high-value HFOs and fluoropolymers, backed by the Chemours strategic partnership and rising specialty chemicals demand.

points: HFO capacity of 10,000 tons commissioning in FY27 with revenue from FY28 — structural transition from low-margin HFCs to premium-priced next-gen refrigerants
Chemours strategic partnership positions SRF as a global fluoropolymer/fluoroelastomer supplier — no upfront capital from Chemours, revenue from CY26
Chemicals segment FY26 revenue grew 22% YoY in Q3FY26 to ₹1,825 crore with 36% EBIT growth; operating leverage improving
15–20% revenue CAGR guidance for FY27 underpinned by new capacity and product launches
Backward integration, indigenous technology, and sole Indian manufacturer of F134a and F32 — durable competitive moat
Net income CAGR of 27% projected over next 4 years per analyst consensus
Expanding pharma propellant and CDMO business diversifies revenue base
bull_case.key_upside: HFO ramp-up and fluoropolymer volumes could drive 30-40% earnings re-rating from FY28

points: Citi reiterated Sell with TP ₹2,725 (13% downside); Jefferies Underperform with TP ₹2,640 (16% downside)
Performance Films segment revenue declined 3% in Q3FY26; Technical Textiles down 11% — two of four segments under pressure
Sharia non-compliant: debt/equity ratio 0.362 exceeds 0.33 threshold
Capex cycle is heavy — ₹2,200–2,300 crore in FY26, additional ₹1,500–2,000 crore in Odisha — payback stretched
Specialty chemicals growth moderated below earlier 20% guidance
Revenue estimates revised down from ₹168.61 bn to ₹164.02 bn for FY26
Agrochemicals demand weakness reduces specialty chemicals offtake
P/E of 42.3x is demanding given near-term execution risks
bear_case.key_downside: Execution delays in HFO/fluoropolymer projects or continued weakness in Films/Textiles could compress valuation multiples

data.verdict: HOLD/SELECTIVE BUY — Quality compounder with visible long-term catalysts but near-term headwinds in non-chemicals segments and rich valuation. Best entry on dips below ₹2,500.
data.sharia_status: NOT COMPLIANT — Debt/equity ratio 0.362 exceeds Islamic finance threshold of 0.33

fluorochemicals.overview: Global fluorochemicals market is transitioning from HFCs (hydrofluorocarbons) to HFOs (hydrofluoroolefins) under the Kigali Amendment to the Montreal Protocol. HFOs have near-zero global warming potential (GWP) versus HFCs with GWP of 1,000-4,000x CO2.
fluorochemicals.hfc_phasedown: Developed nations phasing down HFCs by 80-85% by 2036; India and other developing nations have later timelines (~2024-2047). This drives structural demand for HFOs as substitute refrigerants.
fluorochemicals.hfo_opportunity: HFOs command significant price premium over HFCs — 3-5x pricing. SRF is investing ₹1,100 crore for 10,000 ton HFO capacity commissioning FY27.
fluorochemicals.india_position: India is the only country outside China with indigenous HFC manufacturing capability at scale. SRF is the sole Indian producer of F134a (HFC-134a) and F32 (HFC-32).
fluorochemicals.refrigerant_market: Global refrigerant market size ~$15 billion growing at 6-7% CAGR driven by HVAC-R growth in Asia, automotive AC, and cold chain logistics.

specialty_chemicals.overview: Indian specialty chemicals sector is gaining share from China post-2020 due to China+1 sourcing strategies by Western multinationals, stricter Chinese environmental regulations, and COVID supply chain disruptions.
specialty_chemicals.agrochemicals: India is world 4th largest agrochemical producer; global market ~$250 billion. SRF supplies agrochemical intermediates including fluorinated active ingredients.
specialty_chemicals.pharma: Pharmaceutical CDMO market growing at ~10% CAGR. SRF supplies pharma propellants (HFA 134a) and intermediate synthesis.
specialty_chemicals.market_size: Indian specialty chemicals market estimated at $32 billion, targeting $65 billion by 2035.

packaging_films.overview: BOPET and BOPP flexible packaging films used in FMCG, pharma, food packaging. Oversupply globally from Chinese and Indian capacity additions has pressured margins in FY25-FY26.
packaging_films.outlook: Recovery expected in H2FY27 as capacity additions slow and demand from e-commerce and food sectors grows.

technical_textiles.overview: Nylon tyre cord fabric, polyester tyre cord fabric, belting fabric, coated fabrics. Demand linked to automotive tyre industry.
technical_textiles.trends: Radialisation of tyres in India ongoing; EV transition may shift tyre mix. Chinese competition pressuring margins.

competitive_landscape.srf_moat: Backward integration from HF acid, indigenous technology development, sole manufacturer of F134a in India, Chemours partnership for fluoropolymers

data.overview: SRF Limited is an Indian multi-business chemicals conglomerate incorporated in 1970. Operations span fluorochemicals, specialty chemicals, packaging films, and technical textiles across 11 manufacturing plants in India (Bhiwadi/Rajasthan, Dahej/Gujarat, Odisha upcoming), Thailand, South Africa, and Hungary. Exports to 90+ countries with 8,000+ employees.

chemicals_business.description: Largest and highest-margin segment comprising Fluorochemicals and Specialty Chemicals

products: Floron refrigerants: HFC-134a (F134a), HFC-32 (F32), HFC-125 (F125), HFC blends (410A, 407C)
Pharma propellants: Dymel HFA 134a/P (acquired from DuPont)
Industrial chemicals: AHF (anhydrous hydrofluoric acid), HCFC-22
fluorochemicals.market_position: Only Indian manufacturer of F134a and F32 using indigenous technology; only Indian company with fully integrated HFC blending capability

specialty_chemicals.description: Custom synthesis and CRAMS for agrochemicals and pharmaceuticals; established 2004

performance_films_foil.description: BOPET (biaxially oriented polyethylene terephthalate), BOPP films, aluminum foils for flexible packaging

technical_textiles.description: Nylon tyre cord fabric (NTCF), polyester tyre cord fabric, belting fabrics, coated fabrics

chemours_partnership.scope: SRF to manufacture fluoropolymers and fluoroelastomers for Chemours for global supply
chemours_partnership.capex: ₹745 crore (up from ₹595 crore) — ₹295 crore for Chemours-specific grades (2 products)
chemours_partnership.strategic_value: No upfront capital from Chemours; gives SRF access to Chemours technology and global distribution

data.r_and_d: Deep R&D capability — indigenous development of F134a and F32 technologies; multiple patents in fluorochemicals

sources.title: Business Standard - Ashish Bharat Ram appointed CMD

key_management.name: Ashish Bharat Ram
key_management.title: Chairman & Managing Director
key_management.tenure: MD since January 2007; appointed Chairman & MD from May 23, 2025 to March 31, 2030
key_management.background: Third generation of the Bharat Ram family (founding family of SRF); has led SRF through major diversification into specialty chemicals and fluorochemicals expansion
recognition: AIMA Emerging Business Leader of the Year 2024

key_management.name: Kartik Bharat Ram
key_management.title: Joint Managing Director
key_management.background: Fourth generation of founding family; focuses on technical textiles and operational excellence

board_composition.total_directors: Mix of executive and independent directors
board_composition.promoter_holding: Bharat Ram family (founding family) — promoter group

corporate_governance.disclosures: SEBI LODR compliant; BRSR disclosures with third-party validation
corporate_governance.track_record: Consistent dividend payment history; no major governance controversies on record

management_track_record.value_creation: Stock has delivered multi-bagger returns over 15 years under current leadership
strategic_pivots: Entry and scaling of specialty chemicals (2004)
Acquisition of Dymel HFA from DuPont
management_track_record.capital_allocation: Heavy capex cycle in FY26 (₹2,200–2,300 crore) focused on next-gen fluorochemicals — management has backed greenfield expansions with strong payback history

data.promoter_background: Bharat Ram family has been associated with SRF since its founding in 1970. The company was originally part of the DCM group before becoming independent. Family has maintained significant stake and active operational involvement.

esg_framework.name: Aspirations 2030
esg_framework.description: SRF's long-term ESG commitment framework aligning with UN SDGs
esg_framework.governance_structure: Three-tier ESG governance framework led by ESG Committee chaired by Joint MD

climate_action.strategy: Transitioning product portfolio toward lower-GWP products (HFOs vs HFCs); emissions reduction initiatives across plants
climate_action.hfo_significance: HFOs have near-zero global warming potential vs HFCs (GWP 1,000-4,000x CO2) — SRF's HFO expansion is both a business and climate initiative
climate_action.carbon_reporting: ISO 14064 compliant carbon footprint measurement and reporting

water_management.approach: Closed-loop water management systems at key facilities; Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) implementation
water_management.goal: Increased water recycling reducing freshwater intake

environmental.resource_efficiency: Green chemistry principles integrated in specialty chemicals R&D; responsible consumption and production practices
environmental.chemical_safety: Manufacturing of refrigerants that are ozone-friendly (F134a, F32 are non-ozone depleting vs legacy CFC refrigerants)

social.workforce: 8,000+ employees across 11 manufacturing plants in 4 countries
social.safety: Dedicated Director for Safety & Environment on board (Pramod Gopaldas Gujarathi)
social.community: CSR initiatives in communities around manufacturing plants (Bhiwadi, Dahej)
social.employee_development: Training programs; diversity initiatives under CHRO leadership

governance.transparency: Annual reports, quarterly results, investor presentations publicly available
governance.ethics: Code of conduct; no major fraud/corruption incidents on record
governance.related_party: Promoter family involvement monitored through independent oversight

environmental_risks: Chemical manufacturing inherently carries spill/contamination risk
HFC production itself contributes to greenhouse gases — transition to HFO is positive but requires execution
Regulatory risk from accelerated phase-down schedules
social_risks: Industrial safety incidents possible in chemical manufacturing
governance_risks: Family-controlled company — potential for concentrated decision-making

positive_esg_factors: Only Indian manufacturer of non-ODS (non-ozone depleting) refrigerants
Active transition to lower-GWP products (HFO expansion)
Strong ESG governance structure with board-level oversight

estimate_revisions.fy26_revenue_revision: Revised down from ₹16,861 crore to ₹16,402 crore over past 90 days
estimate_revisions.fy27_revenue_revision: Revised down from ₹19,334 crore to ₹18,835 crore over past 90 days
estimate_revisions.beat_history: SRF has historically beaten revenue estimates by ~2% on average

valuation_context.current_pe_vs_history: 42.3x — mid-range; de-rated from peaks of 55-60x in FY22
valuation_context.peer_comparison: Premium to generalist chemical companies; discount to pure-play specialty chemical peers like Navin Fluorine
valuation_context.fair_value_intrinsic_note: Multiple brokerages flag stretched valuation at current levels given 3-year sales CAGR of only 2%

annual_revenue_cr.note: FY2026 based on Screener consolidated data; official figure per prior data point is ₹15,416 crore

revenue_cagr.note: 3yr CAGR depressed by FY24 trough due to chemical cycle downturn

segment_mix_trend.chemicals_share: Increasing — was ~35% of revenue in FY22, approaching ~50% in FY26
segment_mix_trend.films_share: Declining relative share due to margin pressure
segment_mix_trend.strategic_direction: Management actively shifting mix toward high-margin chemicals

key_revenue_drivers: Refrigerant volumes — record sales in Q2FY26
Specialty chemicals custom synthesis order book
revenue_headwinds: BOPET/BOPP film pricing under pressure from Chinese exports
Tyre cord fabric demand softer from auto sector
Agrochemical channel destocking impacting specialty chemicals offtake

sources.title: ICICI Direct SRF Research Report Q2FY26
SRF Q2 FY26 Earnings Call Highlights

near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.description: Production under Chemours partnership for fluoropolymers/fluoroelastomers commences CY26. No upfront capex from Chemours; SRF bears ₹295 crore capex for Chemours-specific grades.
near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.impact: New high-margin revenue stream; validates SRF as global fluoropolymer manufacturer
near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.magnitude: Moderate — building to meaningful contribution in FY27-28

near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.description: Record refrigerant sales volumes in Q2FY26 indicate strong demand recovery post-HFC channel destocking. Summer seasonality benefits Q1/Q2.
near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.impact: Chemicals segment EBIT leverage — operating profit grew 36-96% YoY in Q2-Q3FY26

near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.description: Agrochemical channel destocking cycle expected to end; new product launches in pharma CDMO segment
near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.impact: Specialty chemicals revenue recovery to support overall chemicals segment growth
near_term_catalysts_fy26_fy27.magnitude: Moderate — dependent on global agrochemical demand

medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.description: 10,000 ton HFO capacity at ₹1,100 crore capex. HFOs are next-generation refrigerants with near-zero GWP replacing HFCs under Kigali Amendment.
medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.impact: Transformational — HFOs priced 3-5x HFCs; high margin product line with structural tailwind
medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.magnitude: Very High — single largest medium-term earnings driver

medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.description: New manufacturing site with ₹1,500–2,000 crore additional capex planned for HFO and specialty chemicals scale-up
medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.impact: Long-term capacity base for next decade of growth

medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.description: Reducing dependency on agrochemicals by building pharma CDMO book; higher margin and more stable than agro
medium_term_catalysts_fy27_fy28.impact: Diversification and margin improvement in specialty chemicals

key_risks.description: Technology complexity of HFO manufacturing is significant. Any delay in commissioning (expected FY27) pushes revenue to FY29+ and weighs on valuation re-rating
Continued Chinese overcapacity in BOPET/BOPP could further pressure realisations; films segment revenue declined 3% in Q3FY26
Management has moderated guidance below earlier 20% CAGR; agro destocking prolonged; pharma offtake uncertain
P/E of 42.3x is demanding if earnings growth disappoints. Major brokerages (Citi, Jefferies) maintain Sell/Underperform
Fluorspar (key raw material for HF acid) price volatility; energy costs at Dahej and Bhiwadi plants
₹2,200–2,300 crore FY26 capex plus Odisha site investments increases leverage. Debt/equity already at 0.362 (above Sharia threshold)
EV transition may reduce tyre cord demand long-term; Chinese competition ongoing
key_risks.probability: Medium but low financial impact given small share

upcoming_events.note: Board decided to announce FY26 results and dividend on May 5, 2026

data.reason: SRF Limited is a diversified industrial conglomerate with meaningful exposure to cyclical end-markets. Its Chemicals segment (refrigerants/HFCs) is subject to global commodity pricing cycles and regulatory-driven demand shifts, while Performance Films and Technical Textiles are tied to industrial capex and auto/packaging demand cycles. The 3-year revenue CAGR of only 2% versus a 10-year CAGR of 13% starkly illustrates the depth of the FY2023-24 downturn, where chemicals pricing collapsed globally. The multi-segment structure provides some diversification, but does not eliminate cyclicality — all segments except packaging faced headwinds simultaneously during the FY2024 trough, confirming high cross-segment correlation during macro downturns.
data.sector_basis: Specialty and commodity chemicals are inherently cyclical due to capacity-driven price cycles, global trade sensitivity, and linkage to industrial production. Refrigerants face additional regulatory cycles (HFC phase-downs under Kigali Amendment), creating boom-bust pricing dynamics. Technical textiles and films are tied to auto OEM production and industrial packaging demand, both of which are pro-cyclical.

quantitative_signals.note: High coefficient of variation driven by FY2024 trough after FY2023 peak; reflects chemicals price cycle
Above-market beta reflects industrial/chemicals exposure; stock amplifies broader market moves
Large fixed asset base in chemicals and films manufacturing means margins compress sharply on revenue declines
PAT swung significantly FY2023-24 as chemicals margins collapsed; multi-year earnings reset observed
FY2023 peak to FY2024 trough across consolidated revenue; Chemicals and Films/Textiles declined simultaneously

cycle_drivers: Global refrigerant (HFC/HFO) pricing cycles tied to inventory restocking and regulatory phase-downs
Chinese capacity expansions creating oversupply in fluorochemicals and driving global price collapses
Industrial capex cycles driving demand for technical textiles (tyre cord, belting fabrics)
Packaging films demand tied to FMCG/food capex and consumer spend cycles
Commodity input costs (fluorspar, chlorine, ethylene) creating margin volatility
defensive_factors: Diversification across 4 segments reduces single-cycle concentration risk
Long-term customer contracts in Technical Textiles provide some revenue visibility
Packaging Films tied to relatively defensive food packaging demand
Ongoing shift toward HFO (next-gen refrigerants) creates structural growth layer above commodity cycle
10-year revenue CAGR of 13% demonstrates underlying secular growth despite cycles
data.historical_cycle: FY2023 represented a peak driven by post-COVID restocking and elevated HFC refrigerant prices; by FY2024 global fluorochemical oversupply (led by China) caused a sharp correction — SRF revenue grew only ~2% over FY2022-FY2024. Q3FY26 Chemicals recovery of +22% signals the next upcycle beginning, while Films (-3%) and Textiles (-11%) remain in their own troughs, illustrating segment-level asynchrony.
data.investor_implication: Buy SRF during chemicals downcycles when Chemicals EBIT margins are compressed below 15% and inventory destocking is near completion — typically 12-18 months after a Chinese oversupply event. Avoid initiating at peak Chemical margins (>22%) or peak revenue growth. Size moderately (5-6%) given diversification, and monitor refrigerant spot prices, China HFC export data, and global auto production as leading indicators.
